findings.description: The full slide is rendered as a composite screenshot image AND the same content is also rendered as separate HTML text, creating a duplication that interrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a single slide with audio troubleshooting on the left and welcome message on the right. The HTML renders a large screenshot image of this entire slide AND separately renders the same text content (AUDIO TROUBLESHOOTING instructions, Welcome heading, date, chat instructions) as HTML text above and below the image.

findings.description: PDF page 3 appears to contain approximately 4 distinct stacked photographs along the right side (bird/egret, marine organisms, a sea creature close-up, and a fish near reef). The HTML only provides 3 tags to represent these, potentially missing one photograph.
findings.evidence: HTML has 3 img tags in the timeline section: 'Photograph of an egret in a wetland', 'Photographs of an oyster reef, scallop, and sandy sea bottom', 'Photograph of a fish swimming near a reef'. The PDF page 3 right column shows approximately 4 distinct stacked photos.

findings.description: Alt text for the timeline images may be overly specific or potentially fabricated, as the exact contents of the photos (e.g., 'scallop', 'sandy sea bottom') cannot be verified from the PDF text alone and may not accurately describe what is depicted.
findings.evidence: HTML alt text: 'Photographs of an oyster reef, scallop, and sandy sea bottom' — the PDF provides no captions or text labels for these photographs, so the specificity of 'scallop' and 'sandy sea bottom' appears to be inferred rather than sourced from the document.
